name: Magic Swords
description: Alone among magic weapons, swords have human — and superhuman — attributes: an alignment (d% 01-65 Lawful, 66-90 Neutral, 91-00 Chaotic; reversed for the life-draining sword), an Intelligence (d12: 7+ brings Primary Powers and communication, 10+ speech and languages, 12 an Extraordinary Ability and telepathy), and — at Intelligence 7+ — an Egoism of 1-12. A sword of wrong alignment wounds its taker: 2 dice between Law and Chaos, 1 die between Neutrality and either (half for a directed non-player character, who may also be freed, converted, or lost to his master). If the sword's Intelligence+Egoism tops the taker's by 6 or more it controls him outright, converting him to its own alignment. An egoistic sword schemes: leading its user past better weapons, into danger for glory, into capture by worthier hands, or surrender to lesser ones it can rule; it demands treasure — scabbards, jewels, guardian magics. In key situations run the influence check (sword total vs character total, see table). Origin/Purpose: on 91+ (d%) the sword bears a mission, its Intelligence and Ego leap to maximum, and it gains a power by origin — Law paralyzes Chaotic opponents, Neutrality +1 to all saving throws, Chaos disintegrates Lawful opponents — applied only against its endowed prey or their servants (general-purpose missions apply to any opposed side). Purposes: slay Magic-Users / Clerics / Fighting-Men / Monsters, defeat Law / defeat Chaos. A purposed sword is always at its task; a user working against it forces an immediate influence check. Bonuses to hit apply always; damage bonuses only for the +2/+3-vs-specific-creature swords, against those creatures.
special_notes: all Primary/Extraordinary functions pass to the user
